title: cleistocactus humboldtii
intro: Cleistocactus humboldtii, also known as the Humoldt's columnar cactus or Humoldt's columnar cactus, is a species of cactus in the genus Cleistocactus. It is a small, columnar cactus that is native to the Andes mountains in South America, known for its bright red flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving pain. Cleistocactus humboldtii can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by boiling its roots and stems in water, although its herbal preparations are not widely recognized or standardized.
